basic.file_name: Feng - Devil horns and a halo.wav

top_problems: Clipping detected: 16616 samples are near/over the limit.
Mud range is elevated around 250-500 Hz.
Track is extremely loud for the selected style and may be crushed.
Dynamic range is tight, which can make the beat feel flattened.
Drum movement is very active; the beat could become overcrowded.
suggested_fixes: Lower the master/output, then use a soft clipper or limiter with safer ceiling.
Back off limiting/clipping only if the punch or clarity disappears.
Reduce over-compression or clipping on the master and let transients breathe.
Gently cut 250-500 Hz on melodies, pads, or the master if the mix feels cloudy.
Mute extra percussion in some sections so the hook/drop feels bigger.
artist_notes: Autotune/key starting point: C Minor. Confidence: 45.6%.
Vocal space estimate: 2.5/10. Higher means easier space for vocals.
Beat leans dark, so brighter vocals/adlibs may cut through well.
producer_notes: Low-end presence looks usable for the selected style.
Drum activity is high at 5.73 hits/sec.
master_notes: Clipping is the first thing to fix before judging the rest of the mix.
Peak: 0.00 dB. RMS: -5.08 dB. Dynamic range: 5.09 dB.
next_steps: Lower the master/output, then use a soft clipper or limiter with safer ceiling.
